to various national news services to the effect that he, [REDACTED]
in this report. [REDACTED] stated that he had known [REDACTED] for
quite a period of time, having had relations with [REDACTED] on various
occasions, due to the fact that both he, [REDACTED] and [REDACTED]
were private fliers and frequently got together to talk shop. [REDACTED]
stated that as far as he was concerned anything [REDACTED] said could be
taken very seriously and that he, [REDACTED], actually believed that Mr.
Arnold had seen the aforementioned flying disks. [REDACTED] stated that
after [REDACTED] reported having seen the flying disks, that the editor
of the paper had assigned him, [REDACTED], the assignment of taking the
northwest area. The results of this assignment to [REDACTED] and what
he subsequently saw is put forth in a sworn statement signed by [REDACTED]

MEMORANDUM FOR THE OFFICER IN CHARGE:
1. On 12 July 1947, [REDACTED] of the United Airlines, was

interviewed at the Boise Municipal Airport, Boise, Idaho. [REDACTED] was passing through Boise on a schedule flight at the time and had a 20 minute stop-over. [REDACTED] reiterated the statements originally made by him to the press as to what he had seen in the late evening of July 4th, when 8 minutes out of Boise on the route to Seattle, Washington. It is the opinion of the interviewer that due to the position [REDACTED] occupies, that he, [REDACTED] would have to be very strongly convinced that he actually saw flying disks before he would open himself for the ridicule attached to a report of this type.
PAUL I. [unclear], S/A, CIC 4th AF

FLYING DISCS SIGHTED BY [REDACTED] AND [REDACTED], TACOMA, WN.,
SM DASH X . REMYTEL AUGUST SEVEN LAST. [REDACTED] UNITED AIRLINES
PILOT, INTERVIEWED RE ASSOCIATIONS WITH [REDACTED]
AND [REDACTED] IN TACOMA, WN., FROM JULY THIRTYONE TO AUGUST THIRD
LAST. [REDACTED] HAS RECEIVED PREVIOUS PUBLICITY FOR HAVING SEEN FLYING
PILOT, [REDACTED] DURING ROUTENE UNITED AIRLINES FLIGHT OUT OF
BOISE, IDAHO. [REDACTED] RELATES THAT HE WENT TO TACOMA, WN., FROM SEATTLE
[REDACTED] AT TACOMA. [REDACTED] CLAIMS HE HAD PREVIOUSLY MET ARNOLD
WEEKS AGO WHILE HE, [REDACTED] WAS PASSING THROUGH ON ROUTINE FLIGHT. ON
THIS LATTER OCCASION, [REDACTED] WAS IN COMPANY OF [REDACTED]
FLYING DISKS SIGHTED BY [REDACTED] AND [REDACTED], TACOMA,
[REDACTED] AND [REDACTED] TACOMA, IN SIGNED STATEMENT INSTANT DATE
A CIGAR BOX OF THESE ROCK FORMATIONS THEY SENT TO [REDACTED] EDITOR OF
[REDACTED] IN EVANSTON, ILLINOIS. THEY STATE [REDACTED] IS ALSO
EDITOR OF [REDACTED] CHICAGO, ILLINOIS. THEY STATE THEY REQUESTED
[REDACTED] ONLY TO MAKE A CHEMICAL ANALYSIS OF THE ROCK FORMATION. b7c
[REDACTED] AND [REDACTED] STATE [REDACTED] LATER WROTE ASKING FOR ADDITIONAL SAMPLES
STATING HE HAD BEEN UNABLE TO ANALYZE THE MATERIAL. [REDACTED] AND [REDACTED]
STATE [REDACTED] A FEW DAYS AFTER THE FLYING DISK STORIES STARTED THE LATTER
HIM WAS FRAGMENTS OF A FLYING DISK. [REDACTED] STATES HE WROTE [REDACTED] A
LETTER IN WHICH HE STATED THE MATERIAL WAS PART OF A FLYING DISK. [REDACTED]
[REDACTED] AND [REDACTED] ADMIT THIS STATEMENT WAS ENTIRELY FALSE. [REDACTED] AND [REDACTED]
STATE THEY WERE CALLED BY [REDACTED] FROM BOISE, IDAHO TO MEET WITH
LAST. THEY STATE [REDACTED] CALLED IN ARMY INTELLIGENCE OFFICERS FROM
[REDACTED] RELATES THAT HE AND [REDACTED] MADE SUBSEQUENT ATTEMPTS TO GO
OUT TO MAURI ISLAND WITH [REDACTED] ON FRIDAY, SATURDAY AND SUNDAY,
AUGUST FIRST, SECOND AND THIRD, BUT WERE PUT OFF BY [REDACTED]
FOR VARIOUS REASONS, [REDACTED] STATES THAT REPORTER [REDACTED] OF TACOMA TIMES
AND [REDACTED] OF UNITED PRESS OFFICE TACOMA INFORMED HIM AND [REDACTED] OF THE
THESE ANONYMOUS CALLS, [REDACTED] BELIEVES THEY WERE MADE BY EITHER [REDACTED]
[REDACTED] ADMITS CALLING HIS PERSONAL FRIEND [REDACTED]
SMITH WOULD LET [REDACTED] IN ON ANY FLYING DISC STORIES WHICH HE MIGHT RUN
INTO. [REDACTED] ALSO ADMITS THAT HE TOLD REPORTER [REDACTED] OF IDAHO STATESMAN
FATAL FLIGHT. [REDACTED] SAYS THAT [REDACTED] TOLD HIM ON SATURDAY NITE AUGUST b7c
SECOND LAST THAT HE, [REDACTED] IF CONTACTED BY THE ARMY OR OTHER AUTHORITIES
WAS GOING TO SAY THAT THE ENTIRE STORY WAS A HOAX. [REDACTED] STATES THAT
HE TOLD HIS COMPLETE STORY TO MAJOR [REDACTED] OF MCCORD FIELD, TACOMA, ON
SUNDAY MORNING, AUGUST THIRD LAST, AND THAT [REDACTED] LEFT TACOMA.
UNLESS ADVISED TO CONTRARY, [REDACTED] WHO ARE BOTH TACOMA
HAMILTON FIELD, CALIFORNIA, AND CAPTAIN [REDACTED] OF UNITED AIR
LINES, SEATTLE, WASHINGTON TO ATTEND THE MEETING. [REDACTED] AND [REDACTED]
ANT BROWN, AS WELL AS [REDACTED] AND [REDACTED] EXACTLY HOW THEY GOT
[REDACTED] AND [REDACTED] STATE THEY FURNISHED SOME OF THE ROCK FORMATION TO THE
INTELLIGENT OFICERS AS SAMPLES. CAPTAIN DAVIDSON AND LIEUTEANT BROWN
[REDACTED], ASSOCIATED PRESS WIREMAN, TACOMA, ADVISED THAT TWO OR THREE
DAYS AFTER THE FLYING DISK STORIES STARTED, HE CONTACTED [REDACTED] TO CHECK
A STORY THE SEATTLE POST INTELLIGENCER HAD GOTEN FRM THE FIRE CHIEF AT
[REDACTED] WASHINGTON, TO THE EFFECT THAT [REDACTED] HAD SOME FLYING DISK
FRAGMENTS. AT THIS TIME [REDACTED] ADMITTED TO [REDACTED] THE ENTIRE STORY WAS
FALSE. [REDACTED] AND [REDACTED] STATE [REDACTED] WAS PAID BY [REDACTED] OF THE
[REDACTED] AND POSSIBLY THE BOISE STATEMAN [unclear] NEWSPAPER, TO COME
FLYING DISK-FRAGMENTS. THEY FURTHER STATE THAT [REDACTED] HAD A
②
FRIEND ON THE CHICAGO TIMES AND WAS POSSIBLY SELLING THE STORY TO THE
CHICAGO TIMES THROUGH THIS FRIEND. A CHECK OF THE RECORDS AT THE b7c
WITHROP HOTEL, TACOMA WHERE [REDACTED] HAD HIS ROOM, REFLECT THAT ON JULY
THIRTY FIRST, LAST, [REDACTED] CALLED [REDACTED] AT A. N. [REDACTED]
EVANSTON, ILLINOIS, COLLECT, AND ALSO CALLED [REDACTED] AT
BOISE, IDAHO, COLLECT. ON AUGUST ONE, LAST, [REDACTED] CALLED [REDACTED] AT
DEARBORN [REDACTED] CHICAGO, COLLECT, AND [REDACTED] CALLED
[REDACTED] AT DEARBORN TWO THREE TWO THREE. ON-JULY THIRTY FIRST AND
SABOTAGED WHICH KILLED CAPTAIN DAVIDSON AND LIEUTEANT BROWN INFERRING
THAT THIS WAS DONE BECAUSE THE INTELLIGENE OFFICERS WERE CARRYING DISK
FRAGMENTS IN THEIR PLANE. [REDACTED] AND [REDACTED] STATE THESE CALLS COULD ONLY
HAVE COME FROM THEMSELVES, [REDACTED] AND DENY THAT THEY MADE THE
CALLS. NO RECORD OF THE CALLS AS HAVING BEEN MADE FROM [REDACTED] ROOM
IN THE WINTHROP HOTEL CAN BE LOCATED. [REDACTED] AND [REDACTED] ARE EVASIVE AS
TO JUST WHAT INFORMATION THEY DID FURNISH BY PHONE OR LETTER TO [REDACTED]
[REDACTED] UNITED PRESS WIRE MAN [REDACTED] TACOMA, WASHINGTON,
STATE IT APPEARS THE BOISE STATEMAN-S PAPER AND [REDACTED] HAVE

Reference is made to the memorandum from Mr. Baughman to Mr. [REDACTED] Harbo, dated August 28, 1947, requesting advice as to the final disposi- tion of material submitted by Mrs. [REDACTED] Estill, South Carolina.
It is noted that this material, which [REDACTED] reported to be a part of a flying saucer, has been identified as powdered soap stones.
It is recommended that the material be returned to Mrs. [REDACTED] along with a letter advising that such has been identified as powdered soap stones.

For the Bureau's information a Mrs. [REDACTED] of Saybrook, Illinois reported to this office the finding of a flying disc in her front yard at 6:00 A.M. on July 26, 1947.

It appears from investigation conducted by an Agent of this office that the stability of [REDACTED] is questionable. However the alleged flying disc was obtained and it is apparently the concoction of some of the juveniles in the area. It is an old wooden platter, which has assembled on it a silver plate, a spark plug, a timer, and some old brass tubing. Photographs were taken of the same and there are six views enclosed herewith.

Reference is made to my letter to the Bureau dated July 28, 1947 entitled as above forwarding a copy of a report furnished to me by Lieutenant [REDACTED] Fourth Air Force, Hamilton Field, California, setting forth the results of inquiries made by his office concerning the reported sightings of flying discs.
